title: Uncrafting Recipes
description: Uncraft nearly all vanilla items in the game
body:
## This mod allows you to uncraft the majority of <u>vanilla</u> items:
The latest version has 768 uncrafting recipes.

As the mod adds a lot of recipes, if you have a recipe conflict with other mods, I recommend installing Polymorph.

## I know what you are thinking:

- "Can I uncraft slabs into blocks?" Yes.

- "Why do some uncrafting recipes (stairs and copper blocks) look like a scam?" Because they give back the same amount of resources that you would have used if you crafted them using a stonecutter.

- "Why only vanilla recipes?" Generally, what makes an uncrafting table broken is the fact that it can uncraft anything, limiting the uncrafting to vanilla items and not giving back 100% of ingredients for some recipes won't break the game too much. In a modpack, you can see this mod as an early game uncrafting and then you can use an uncrafting table for mid-end game.

- "How to add/remove/modify a recipe?" Recipes are data-driven, every recipe can be modified through datapack.

## Uncrafting recipes that you won't (probably) be able to see in JEI:

- all tipped arrows to normal arrow

- all potions (except bad omen) to glass bottle

## How it works:

If an item is crafted with only 1 item, it will be uncrafted giving back the same amount of materials, like this:

![example 1](https://i.imgur.com/9ntDEu7.png)

If an item is crafted with 2 or more items and 1 is more valuable than the others, it will be uncrafted into the most valuable, like this:

![example 2](https://i.imgur.com/YHml2Ti.png)

If an item is crafted with 2 or more items and an item is not clearly more valuable than the others, there will be different uncrafting recipes, like this:

![example 3](https://i.imgur.com/dBFOlA0.png)

## Feedback
This mod also exists on curseforge, for any problem, leave a comment on the [curseforge project](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/uncraftingrecipes).
